title: U.S. SEC nearing settlement with Wall Street firms over WhatsApp probe -sources
text: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is finalizing settlements with around two dozen Wall Street firms to resolve investigations into record-keeping lapses, said two people with knowledge of the matter.

title: My $12,000 Investment Is Already Paying Bigger Dividends
text: A reasonable payout ratio often gives companies room to increase their dividends. When a company has increased its dividend on a once-a-year pace in the past, it could very well do so again.

title: 5 Stocks With Recent Dividend Hike in a Volatile September
text: Dividend-paying stocks should be in demand as investors try to safeguard their portfolios. Five such stocks are: PECO, VZ, FITB, EG, IDCC.

title: Fifth Third (FITB) Announces a 6% Hike in Quarterly Dividend
text: Fifth Third (FITB) has announced a 6% increase in its dividend. Regular dividend hikes are likely to enhance shareholder value.

title: Fifth Third Bancorp Increases Quarterly Cash Dividend on its Common Shares 2 cents, or 6%, to $0.35 per Share
text: CINCINNATI--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Today Fifth Third Bancorp announced the declaration of cash dividends on its common shares, Series H preferred shares, Series I preferred shares, Series J preferred shares, Series K preferred shares, Series L preferred shares, and Class B Series A preferred shares. Fifth Third Bancorp (Nasdaq: FITB) today declared a cash dividend on its common shares of $0.35 per share for the third quarter of 2023. The dividend is payable on October 16, 2023 to shareholders of reco.

title: Why these investors love bank stocks
text: Banks currently are the industry group that is most popular with the top-performing newsletters that my auditing firm monitors.

title: Fifth Third Bank Certifies 25 Graduates of its Empowering Community Leaders Program
text: CINCINNATI--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Fifth Third congratulates the 25 community leaders who have completed the Bank's 18-month Empowering Community Leaders career development growth program. A Harvard Kennedy School Executive Leadership Certification Ceremony was held at Fifth Third Center in Cincinnati on August 28. “We developed this program for the simple reason that many community leaders don't have the resources to spend on their own development,” said Kala Gibson, chief corporate responsibility o.
